description: Baseline: 106ms total. Large graph routing dominates at 74ms. Node lookups 5ms.

asi.hypothesis: Baseline measurement
asi.bottleneck: A* routing is 90% of cost. Large graph: 74ms routing out of 82ms cycle. Priority queue + obstacle lookup optimization should be highest impact.

description: Binary heap for A* open set + gScores map. Small improvement in cycle times (82→80ms large).

asi.hypothesis: Binary heap reduces O(n log n) sort to O(log n) extraction
asi.finding: Cycle time improved slightly but route_large went up - the gScores Map adds overhead. Main bottleneck is isBlocked/isPathBlocked linear scan through obstacles, called thousands of times per A* run.
asi.next_action_hint: Most edges in grid have direct clear path (neighbors), but isPathBlocked still scans all obstacles. Need spatial indexing for obstacle checks OR early-exit optimization.

description: Spatial hash grid for obstacle lookups. 106→14.6ms (7.3x speedup). Large routing 74→3.25ms.

asi.hypothesis: Spatial hash grid turns O(n) obstacle checks into O(1) amortized
asi.finding: Massive 7.3x improvement. Routing went from dominant cost to secondary. Now lookup_large (5ms) is comparable to cycle_large (9.45ms), meaning node.find() is a significant fraction of remaining cost.
asi.next_action_hint: Use Map for node lookups in benchFullCycle instead of array.find(). Also optimize useSolmu to use Map.

description: Node Map for O(1) lookups + spatial grid in useSolmu. 14.6→5.45ms. Eliminated per-edge obstacle filter array allocation.

asi.hypothesis: Map lookup O(1) vs array.find O(n) per edge
asi.finding: 14.6→5.45ms, another 2.7x. cycle_large down from 9.45 to 3.35ms. Now route_large (3.29ms) is close to cycle_large (3.35ms) — most cycle time IS routing now. lookup_large still 5ms (separate bench, not optimized yet).
asi.next_action_hint: Look at reducing allocations in A* (object creation), string key generation. Also isSegmentBlocked creates Set per call.

description: Generation counter replaces Set in isSegmentBlocked. 5.45→5.43ms (within noise but avoids GC pressure).

asi.hypothesis: Replace per-call Set with generation counter in isSegmentBlocked
asi.finding: Marginal improvement. Most A* calls resolve via the direct-path check at the top and never enter the main loop. The remaining time is in path simplification, SVG string building, and the overhead of building maps/grids.
asi.next_action_hint: Profile where time goes in cycle: is it the Map construction, grid construction, or routing itself? Could try reducing string allocations in SVG path building.

description: Optimize getNodeBounds (avoid temp arrays), SVG path array join, avoid per-edge config spread. 5.43→5.30ms.

asi.hypothesis: Reduce allocations in getNodeBounds, SVG path building, and per-edge config spread
asi.finding: bounds_large 5x faster (0.10→0.02ms). Total improved marginally. The remaining 3.25ms cycle_large is dominated by calculateRoute calls, which for adjacent nodes mostly just do the direct-path-clear check.
asi.next_action_hint: Try connector lookup optimization (build connector map per node), or optimize the direct-path check in SpatialGrid. Also consider if simplifyPath/snapToAxis adds overhead for the common 2-point path case.

description: Fast path for 2-point direct routes. No improvement (5.30→5.40ms, within noise but slightly worse).

asi.hypothesis: Skip simplifyPath/orthogonalizePoints for 2-point direct paths
asi.rollback_reason: No improvement - the 2-point case already exits early from simplifyPath/orthogonalizePoints. The overhead is elsewhere.
asi.next_action_hint: Profile more precisely. The 3.25ms cycle_large includes: ~0.02ms bounds + spatial grid construction + 1740 * (node lookup + connector find + calculateRoute + segment computation). Try reducing midpointOfPolyline/endpointLabelPoints allocations, or optimize the spatial grid cell lookup.

description: Numeric hash keys for spatial grid (avoid string allocation). 5.30→5.02ms.

asi.hypothesis: Numeric hash keys avoid string allocation in spatial grid lookups
asi.finding: 5.3→5.02ms (~5% improvement). route_large 3.43→3.03ms. Still dominated by per-edge overhead: calculateRoute invocation, midpointOfPolyline, endpointLabelPoints, SVG path string generation.
asi.next_action_hint: Try inlining the direct-path result construction for the common 2-point case to reduce function call overhead. Or pre-compute connector positions per node.

description: Numeric hash keys for A* closedSet and gScores. 5.02→4.43ms (12% improvement).

asi.hypothesis: Replace string keys with numeric hash in A* closedSet/gScores
asi.finding: 4.43ms total. route_large 3.03→2.63ms. String→number keys consistently help across all map/set usages. We're now at 24x faster than baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: For the cycle benchmark: spatial grid construction happens every render. Could try caching it. Also midpointOfPolyline creates objects per call. Or try reducing object allocations in calculateRoute for the 2-point direct path.

description: Inline fast path for 2-point direct routes (skip simplify/midpoint/endpoint overhead). 4.43→2.89ms (35% improvement, 37x from baseline).

asi.hypothesis: Inline label/path computation for 2-point case to avoid function call overhead + object allocations
asi.finding: 2.89ms total (37x from baseline). cycle_large 2.73→1.70ms. route_large 2.63→1.53ms. The remaining 1.70ms for 1740 edges is ~1µs per edge. Getting close to V8 overhead floor.
asi.next_action_hint: Try: 1) Avoid allocating new Point objects in isSegmentBlocked (p1/p2 creation), 2) Pre-build connector lookup maps per node, 3) Try simpleOrthogonalRoute before A* for cases where no obstacles in bounding box.

description: Zero-allocation lineIntersectsRectXY + segIntersectXY. Regressed 2.89→3.40ms. V8 may not inline large functions.

asi.hypothesis: Avoid object allocation in lineIntersectsRect by passing raw coordinates
asi.rollback_reason: Regressed. The coordinate-passing approach likely exceeds V8's inlining heuristic (too many parameters). The existing Point-based approach is actually fine because V8 can optimize small short-lived objects with escape analysis.
asi.next_action_hint: Try smaller optimizations: reduce allocations in isSegmentBlocked (reuse Point objects), or optimize the SpatialGrid cell size. Also try connector pre-indexing.

description: Skip segment check in A* for single-step moves. Regressed 2.89→3.80ms (invalid paths cause longer A* searches).

asi.hypothesis: Skip isSegmentBlocked in A* - isPointBlocked alone should suffice for small steps
asi.rollback_reason: Regressed badly. Without segment check, paths can clip obstacle corners causing longer detours and more A* iterations. The segment check catches diagonal cuts through obstacle margins.
asi.next_action_hint: Try: pre-compute connector positions into a lookup map per node. Or optimize spatial grid cell size. Or reduce object allocations in the hot isSegmentBlocked path.

description: Cell size benchmark - 20 is already optimal. No change to commit.

asi.hypothesis: Test different spatial grid cell sizes
asi.rollback_reason: cellSize=20 already optimal. No code change needed.

description: Connector pre-index map + path reverse. Regressed 2.89→4.29ms (map construction cost > savings for 4 connectors).

asi.hypothesis: Pre-build connector map + use push/reverse for A* path
asi.rollback_reason: Connector map string key creation overhead exceeds benefit for 4 connectors/node. push/reverse was neutral (A* rarely runs since most paths are direct).
asi.next_action_hint: Try just the path reverse optimization alone. Or try reducing the spatial grid construction time. Or try a completely different approach: reduce number of edges that need routing.

description: Move direct-path check before grid snapping. Regressed 2.89→3.72ms (V8 optimization sensitivity).

asi.hypothesis: Skip gridStart/gridEnd object creation for direct paths
asi.rollback_reason: Regressed - V8 JIT is sensitive to function shape changes. Moving code around in hot functions can cause deoptimization.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on higher-level optimizations: reduce iteration count, skip unnecessary work. Try increasing benchmark iterations for more stable measurements.

description: Single-cell fast path in isSegmentBlocked. No clear improvement (2.89→2.95ms, high variance).

asi.hypothesis: Fast path for single-cell segment checks (skip dedup, min/max)
asi.rollback_reason: Within noise, not clearly better. The branch cost may offset savings.
asi.next_action_hint: At ~2.9ms we're close to V8 overhead floor. Try reducing benchmark noise with more iterations. Or look at completely different optimizations: SVG path string caching, reduce allocations in calculateRoute return value.

description: Avoid sqrt in simplifyPath collinearity check. Regressed 2.89→3.68ms (simplifyPath rarely runs for direct paths).

asi.hypothesis: Avoid sqrt in simplifyPath using squared comparison
asi.rollback_reason: Regressed. simplifyPath is rarely called (most paths are 2-point direct). Function shape change likely caused V8 deopt.
asi.next_action_hint: Micro-optimizations in routing.ts are hitting diminishing returns at ~2.9ms. Focus on useSolmu-level optimizations or try broader architectural changes.

description: Revert SVG path to string concat. Regressed 2.89→3.95ms (array.join was better).

asi.hypothesis: String concat vs array.join for SVG path building
asi.rollback_reason: array.join is faster than string concat for SVG paths
asi.next_action_hint: Micro-opts exhausted at ~2.9ms. Focus on useSolmu architectural changes or try larger-graph benchmarks.

description: Added xlarge benchmark (2500 nodes, 4900 edges). 5.27ms cycle. Noisy total due to xlarge warmup.

asi.hypothesis: Add 2500-node benchmark
asi.rollback_reason: Discarding because it adds noise to primary metric

description: isLineBlocked wrapper to avoid coordinate unpacking. Regressed 2.89→3.48ms.

asi.hypothesis: Add isLineBlocked wrapper to reuse Point objects
asi.rollback_reason: Extra method dispatch negates savings. V8 already optimizes Point object creation.
asi.next_action_hint: At 2.89ms (37x improvement), routing micro-opts are saturated. Try broader optimizations: renderer lookup map, memoized edge processing.

description: Renderer Map for O(1) type lookups in useSolmu. Benchmark neutral (doesn't test React) but beneficial for real apps.

asi.hypothesis: Replace config.renderers.find() with Map lookup for O(1) renderer resolution
asi.finding: Benchmark neutral since it doesn't test useSolmu's node rendering. But in real apps with many node types, this avoids O(n*m) where n=nodes, m=renderer types.
asi.note: Keeping because it's a genuine improvement for real applications even though benchmark can't measure it.

description: Memoize nodeBoundsCache in useSolmu (was recomputed every render). Benchmark neutral but avoids spatial grid rebuild on state changes.

asi.hypothesis: Memoize nodeBoundsCache to prevent unnecessary recomputation and spatial grid rebuild
asi.finding: Benchmark neutral (simulates per-render cost). In real React apps, this prevents the expensive getNodeBounds + createSpatialGrid from running when only selection/drag state changes - huge win for interactive performance.
asi.note: Primary metric appears worse (3.53 vs 2.89) but this is noise - the change doesn't affect the benchmark's measured code path.
